steps.title: Use the no-solution rule
steps.content:
A linear equation has no solution when the $x$-coefficients match but the constants do not. Expand the left side:
$$
2(kx-n)=2kx-2n
$$
So the left-side $x$-coefficient is $2k$.

steps.title: Match the coefficients
steps.content:
Set the left-side $x$-coefficient equal to the right-side $x$-coefficient:
$$
2k=-\frac{22}{13}
$$
$$
k=-\frac{11}{13}
$$

steps.title: Check the constants
steps.content: With this value, the $x$-terms cancel. Since $n>1$, the constant $-2n$ cannot equal $-\frac{34}{23}$, so the equation has no solution. The answer is -11/13.
